title: Apple333 application phase
objective: Deliver one approved application capability with explicit scope, reusable boundaries, and verifiable quality gates.

inScope: Implement only the approved module, route, component, or API contract.
Add focused automated tests and phase documentation.

decisions.title: Respect module boundaries
decisions.rationale: Keep application work inside the owning module and shared primitives so the enterprise platform remains maintainable.

steps.description: Confirm the approved user outcome, API boundary, and files that may change.
steps.expectedOutcome: A bounded implementation plan with no implicit feature expansion.

steps.description: Implement the approved behavior using existing platform conventions and strict TypeScript.
steps.expectedOutcome: The requested application capability is complete without unrelated refactors.

steps.description: Run required automated and manual checks, then record remaining limitations.

nonGoals: Do not create or apply database migrations.

commands.purpose: Run unit coverage relevant to the application change.

manualChecks: Review user-visible behavior against the approved acceptance criteria.
Confirm forbidden paths and secrets were not changed.

deliverables.description: Records scope, validation evidence, and follow-up work.

risks.description: An apparently small application change can alter an existing storefront or API contract.
risks.mitigation: Use narrow tests and explicitly document affected contracts before implementation.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: The approved capability works and all required checks pass.
No production access, database mutation, or unapproved path change occurs.

stopConditions: Stop when scope requires a database migration, production access, or a protected-path change not explicitly approved.
Stop when existing tests reveal an unrelated regression that cannot be safely isolated.
